departments.name: Administration Company
departments.brief: Administration Company (Admin Company) is a department of the Depot Headquarters. It is charged with coordinating the training support activities of the institution. It also ensures the security of the Depot. To this end, a routine day and night patrol is being conducted. This, by extension has also improved the security of the host communities. It also enforces discipline among the soldiers of the Depot.
departments.hod_bio:
Major Nurudeen Ibraheem is the Officer Commanding Admin Company. He was born on 7th September 1989 in Offa Local Government Area, where he attended Nawair-Ud-Deen Primary School. He had his Secondary education at Offa Grammar School, Offa Kwara State and Okoyelola High School, Konta-Ijabe, Osun State. On 14th
August 2010, Nurudeen got admitted into the Nigerian Defence Academy as a member of 62 Regular Course and was commissioned into the Nigerian Army Corp of Infantry on 12 September 2015.
He has served in various units where he held different appointments. These includes 19 Battalion Okitipupa as a Platoon Commander and Acting Operations Officer between November 2015 – November 2016. 81 Divisaion Task Force Battalion (81 Div TF Bn) Gulumba-Gana and Dikwa in Borno State from 2016 – 2020. At 81 Div TF Bn, he was appointed Operations Officer, Intelligence Officer and the Battalion Adjutant. He also commanded Alfa and Bravo Companies of the Battalion at various times. In March 2020, he was taken on strength at 2 Battalion, Kaduna and appointed the Battalion Adjutant in August.
He was posted as the pioneer operation officer of 2 Special Women Operations Battalion,Abakaliki on 11 January 2011, he was posted to Depot NA Osogbo. 
However, following his promotion to the rank of Major in August the same year, he was redeployed to Admin Coy as the Officer Commanding.
Nurudeen has attended a number of courses which include Young Officers Course 26/17, Basic Surveillance Course 11/23 and Junior Course 95/23. All at the Nigerian Army School of Infantry, Jaji, Kaduna State. Other courses attended includes, Basic Human Right Awareness Course 7/22 at the Martin Lurther Leadership and Peace Keeping Centre also in Jaji among other courses.
He is married and blessed with 3 Children. In his leisure time, he enjoys reading, jugging and listening to the music of Dr SikiruAyinde Barrister.

departments.name: Army Public Relations
departments.brief:
BRIEF ON THE ACTIVITIES OF THE DEPOT ARMY PUBLIC RELATIONS DEPARTMENT OSOGBO
INTRODUCTION
1.	The Depot Nigerian Army Public Relations Department was established in 31 May 2025 as part of the branch directly responsible to the Commandant Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo to carry out public relations related activities on behalf the Institution. The Department in line with its mandate manages information in order to project the image/reputation of the Depot as well as offers media related advise to the Commandant. The Depot NA APR is headed by the Assistant Director Army Public Relations (ADAPR) who also serves as the Liaison/ Protocol Officer of the Depot. The Department current manpower holding on strength is 1 x 6.
FUNCTIONS OF THE DEPARTMENT
2.	The Depot APR headed by the ADAPR who serves as the official spokesperson of the Institution performs the following functions:
a.	Managed relationships with the media and ensuring that all public communications positively reflect the Institution’s image and objectives.
b. 	Coordinate press releases, official statements and responses to media inquiries.
c. 	Oversee the management and security of digital communication
platforms in the Department.
d. 	Provide oversight on the preparation and delivery of official digital
correspondence.
e. 	Assist to manage the Institution’s social media and online presence.
f. 	Ensure strict adherence to NA protocols concerning the posting of
sensitive or classified information online.
g.	Publicize all College activities to ensure maximum visibility and engagement.
h. 	Oversee the production and distribution of banners, flyers and other print media to support informational activities.
i. 	Liaison with civil authorities including the Police and other Para -
military organizations for Public Relations duties.
j. 	Conduct all visits to the Institution and this shall include assisting to receive visitors to the Institution and conducting them to designated places/offices.
k. 	Reception of guests to the Institution and this shall include arrangement of reception, receiving and ushering guests to Commandant’s Office.
l. 	Provide photos/video coverage for all Depot’s activities.
m. 	Carry out any other task that may be assigned.
YA IBRAHIM
Maj
Jun 26								ADAPR
departments.hod_role: Deputy Assistant Director, Army Public Relations
departments.hod_bio:
Major Yahaya Arama Ibrahim was born on the 29 September 1983  into the family of  Late Mal. Abdulrahim Ibrahim. He hails from Okene Local Government Area of Kogi State and was commissioned into the Nigerian Army as Lieutenant on 5 July 2014 as a member of Direct Short Service Course 21. He began his Primary School education at Command Children School Dantuku Road in Kaduna State from 1986- 1992, and thereafter proceeded to Nigerian Military School Zaria from 1992 - 1998. He obtained his National Diploma and Higher National Diploma in Mass Communication from Kaduna Polytechnic, Kaduna State from 2000 - 2006. After which he obtained a Post Graduate Diploma in Mass Communication from the Ahmadu Bello University Zaria, Kaduna State in 2010.
Maj Ibrahim has attended the Young Officer Course Army Public Relations, Media Operations Officers’ Course and Advance Public Relations Course at the Nigerian Army School of Public Relations. He attended the British Military Advisory Training Team CIMIC Operators’ Course at the Martin Luther Agwai International Peace Keeping Center Jaji, Basic Computer Course at Nigerian Army Signal School Apapa Lagos state and 	Junior Leadership and Staff Officers Course (JLSOC) 1/27 at the Nigerian Army College of Logistics and Management Lagos.
Ibrahim has the following Operational Experience which include;United Nations Mission in Liberia, NIBATT 26 and NIBATT 38 in 2013/2016 respectively, Operation Lafiya Dole Maiduguri, Borno State from 2018 - 2020, Operation Sharan Daji/ Operation HADARIN DAJI Zamfara State from 2020 - 2024, Operation Forest Sanity in Katsina State in 2020 and Operation SAHEL SANITY in Sokoto state 2021.
Ibrahim has served in the following formations and held appointments commiserate to his rank, notablly; SO3 Public Administration and    Research at HQ 2 Division APR, Force Public Information Officer at United Nation Mission in Liberia, SO3 Publicity and Information at HQ 81 Division APR, Liaison Officer NAOWA 81 Division Chapter, ADAPR at HQ 29 Task Force Brigade Maiduguri, Ag ADAPR at HQ 1 Brigade Gusau, Zamfara State, Force Information Officer Joint Task Force North West Operation HADARIN DAJI in Sokoto state, Information officer Operation SAHEL Sanity in Katsina State, Ag ADAPR NACEST Makurdi, Benue State and Currently the Pioneer ADAPR Depot NA OSOGBO.
Maj Ibrahim is an Associate Member Nigerian Institute of Public Relations, Member Nigerian Ar

departments.name: Band Department
departments.hod_bio:
Capt Bernard Jibrin Idoko was born on 3 October 1984 into family of the Late LCpl Jibrin Idoko and Mrs Helen Idoko in ingle Edumaga Okpokwu LGA of Benue State. He attended Odissasa Primary School, Ugbokolo from 1990 to 1995. In 1996, he proceeded to Government Secondary School, Okpoga, Benue State, where he obtained his SSCE 2001.
In 2002, Bernard Idoko was enlisted into the Nigerian Army as a Member of the 52 RRI and was posted to the Nigericin Army Band Corps. In 2009, he was sponsored by the Nigerian Army to study music at the University of Ado-Ekiti, Ekiti State, where he bagged a Barchelor of Art (BA Music) in Sept, 2013. Bernard Idoko was commission into the Nigerian Army as a Member of Executive Commission Course 1/17 in 2017, and was posted to the Nigerian Army Band Corps.
As a Commissioned Officer, Capt Bernard Idoko has serve in various units, including: 9 Bde Band as commanding Officer 2019-2020, 31 Arty Bde as commanding Officer 2020-2022, 2 Bde Band as commanding Officer 2022-2025 and Depot NA Band Osogbo where he is presently serving as pioneer commanding officer.

departments.name: Catering Department
departments.brief:
INTRODUCTION
1.	The upsurge in insurgency and criminality across the country necessitated the establishment of Depot NA Osogbo to enable the NA meet the need for sufficient personnel to tackle insecurity and act in aid of civil authority. Consequently, Depot NA Osogbo took off in July 2025 with the training of Recruits of 89 RRI. As a training depot, one of the most important departments essential for effective trainin g of recruits in Depot NA Osogbo is the Cat Dept. As a result, Depot NA Osogbo Cat Dept took off at the inception of the Depot and within this period has made concerted efforts to make its impact in the training of the recruits.
2.	The role of feeding in physically demanding activities such as the training of recruits cannot be over-emphasised. As a well-known saying goes  “A Soldier Marches on His Belly”. In conformity with the above saying, HQ Depot NA Osogbo moved quickly to established a catering department that can stand the test of time and provide adequate nutrition for the recruits. Thus, various kitchen equipment were procured and supplied for effective functioning of the Department while efforts were made to install the various equipment to provide adequate support for efficient training.
ACTIVITIES OF THE CATERING DEPARTMENT
3.	The major activities of the Cat Dept include preparation and provision of adequate and balanced diet for recruits in line with extant NA  regulations on provision of ration to personnel. In line with the above, Depot NA Osogbo Cat Dept carries out the following activities:
a.	Provision of Three Square Meals for Recruits Daily.	 The primary responsibility of the Department is the provision of 3 square meals for feeding of recruits daily as approved by AHQ. Consequently, the Department makes arrangement with food vendors who provide dry rations for the daily maintenance of recruits. The Department vetts and engages the vendors in line with extant NA regulations to guide against unwholesome practices in the supply of dry ration for recruits feeding. Additionally, the Department engages civilian caterers to complement NA personnel due to the level of work involved in feeding the recruits. Currently, 80 civilian staff work in two shifts to support Supply and Transport personnel who are primarily responsible for provision of catering services in all NA activities.
b.	Provision of Lunch for Officers and Soldiers.	In line with the COAS’ directive, all NA personnel in Depot NA Osogbo are provided lunch on week days.
departments.hod_bio:
1.	Lt Col AA Amadi hails from Agba-Ozizza Community in Afikpo North LGA of Ebonyi State. He attended Agba Primary School where he obtained his First School Leaving Certificate and then proceeded to Comprehensive Secondary School, Afikpo for his Senior School Certificate. He thereafter gained admission to the Federal Polytechnic, Unwana-Afikpo where he obtained his National Diploma  and Higher National Diploma in Accounting and Finance.
2.	The Senior Officer was admitted into the NDA where he trained and was commissioned as a member of Short Service Course 34. Upon completion of his cadet training, he was posted to Headquarters Nigerian Army Corps of Supply and Transport (HQ NACST). The Senior Officer was thereafter posted to 	1 Div Tpt Bn as a Pl Comd from where he was nominated as part of NIBATT 10 African Mission in Sudan for peace support operations. Upon return from the peace support operations, Lt Col Amadi was nominated as part of NIBATT 19, United Nations Mission in Liberia (NUMIL).
3.	Lt Col Amadi was depolyed to the NE as Cat Offr 3 Div SOB  on his de-induction from UNMIL as part of the first set of troops to counter insurgency in the NE. The unit later metamorphosed to 27 TF Bde and moved from Damaturu to Buni Yadi. After 3 years in the NE, the Senior Officer was de-inducted and proceeded to the Armed Forces Command and Staff College (AFCSC) for his Junior Course (JC). On completion of JC, Lt Col Amadi was posted to the NACOL as Cat Offr. In February 2017, he was posted to 4 Bde as Ag CO ST Bn. After his tour of duty in Benin, he proceeded to AFCSC for his Senior Course (SC).
4.	On completion of SC at the AFCSC, Lt Col Amadi was posted to AHQ DAPP as SO2 Pcy. After serving for two years in AHQ DAPP, the Senior Officer was posted to 763 ST Bn as CO. He was thereafter posted from 763 ST Bn to HQ NACST in December 2025 and appointed DCOS G4. The Senior was posted to Depot NA Osogbo in January 2026 where he currently serves as Cat Offr.
5.	Lt Col Amadi is married to Mrs Augusta Ubolo Amadi-Ulu and the marriage is blessed with two children. At his leisure, he enjoys swimming, playing Badminton and watching nature movies.

departments.name: Chaplaincy (Roman Catholic)
departments.brief:
BRIEF OF DEPARTMENT OF CHAPLAIN SERVICES (ROMAN CATHOLIC), DEPOT NA OSOGBO.
Lt EO Nwokoye is the pioneer chaplain posted by the Head Quarters Military Secretariat (HQ MS) in June 2025.
POSTED STRENGTH
1 by 3:
Lt EO Nwokoye (N/21016).
97NA/44/2584 Wo  Dakat I.
93NA/36/2493 Ssgt Obasi Osang
08NA/61/1133 Sgt Tobechukwu Ogazi.
MANDATES OF CHAPLAIN SERVICES (ROMAN CATHOLIC) DEPOT NA OSOGBO
The mandates of the Department of Chaplain Services (Roman Catholic), Depot NA Osogbo is in line with the vision of the Directorate of Chaplain Services (Roman Catholic) Nigerian Army, which is to provide holistic spiritual, moral and psychological support to military personnel and their families for effective performance in line with COAS command philosophy. The mandates of Chaplain Services (Roman Catholic) Depot NA include:
Giving advice to Depot Commandant on administrative and religious matters.
Leading personnel, families and recruits in worship on Sundays and weekdays.
Organizing padre hour on Fridays for personnel and recruits.
Providing spiritual guidance to officers, soldiers, families and recruits.
Administering different sacraments to personnel, their families and recruits.
Providing pastoral guidance for personnel, families and recruits.
Giving moral and spiritual courage to personnel, families and recruits.
Naming ceremony and prayers for new born babies.
Conducting wedding ceremony for personnel.
Funeral prayers and burial of personnel, families and recruits.
Guidance and counseling of personnel, families and recruits.
departments.hod_bio:
1. Lt. Edwin Okechukwu Nwokoye was born on 14 of Dec 1995 into the family of Mr. and Mrs. Joseph Nwokoye. He hails from Orumba North Local Government Area of Anambra State. He is a Christian (Roman Catholic) by religion. He began his primary school Education at All Saints Nursery and Primary School, Awgbu in Anambra State, and obtained his First School Leaving Certificate from Seat of Wisdom Nursery and Primary School, Umunze, Anambra State, in the year 2005. He further proceeded to Holy Child Secondary School, Isuofia, Anambra State, where he obtained his West African Senior School Certificate in the year 2012.
2. Lt Nwokoye holds a Bachelor’s Degree in Philosophy, (B.Phil) from Pope JohnPaul II Major Seminar Awka, Anambra state in 2018. And also Bachelor's Degree in Theology (B.Th) from Blessed Iwene Tansi Major Seminary Onitsha, Anambra State in 2023. Upon completion of his seminary training, he was ordained a Roman Catholic priest for the Catholic Diocese of Awka, on 15 Jul 2023.
3. Lt Edwin was admitted on 10 May 2024 as a member of DSSC 28 to train in NDA and was commissioned into the NA as a Lieutenant on 28 Sep 2024.
4. Lt Edwin was posted to 2 Div NA Ibadan, as SO2 Chap (RC) on 17 Feb 2025. And was later posted to Depot NA Osogbo as the pioneer Chap (RC) on 30 May 2025.
5. Courses attended:
a. Leadership and Regimentaion Course 2024.
b. YOC Chaplain (RC) 2026.
At his leisure, he enjoys reading, football, adventure.

departments.name: Cantonment Maintenance Group
departments.brief:
1.	Cantonment Maintenance Groups (CMG) sustain the physical environment where soldiers live, train, and recover. The functions of the CMG cut across minor civil works, electrical services, plumbing, carpentry, as well as masonry works. Maintenance group also ensures uninterrupted water supply, habitable accommodations, safe roads, and power supply amongst others.
2.	The CMG Depot NA Osogbo, was formed following the establishment of Depot NA Osogbo, on 7 Jun 25. Ab-initio, the group was composed of 1 x 10 soldiers including artisans. However, with subsequent postings and internal redeployments, the group’s manpower holding has raised to 2 x 15 Soldiers at the moment.  The CMG Depot NA Osogbo is a functional department that is saddled with all maintenance services. It is responsible for the maintenance, repair and general engineering services of all the Depot infrastructural facilities, and utilities. The CMG provides technical support to the Depot in order to ensure seamless execution of training activities.
3.	Since the establishment of Depot NA Osogbo, the CMG has undertaken several impactful projects and constructions to improve facilities and enhance living conditions for both recruits and staff within the Depot. These achievements are; the remodeling of Quarter Guard, construction of a stable, installation of solar-powered street lights, construction of a drainage system at the Recruit Center, Training Branch and Record Office Blocks, construction of checkpoints, boom gate, and guard house using hesco bags, as well as jacketing and remodeling of Tangos to include conveniences and rest areas. Others include the construction of the Hall of Fame, construction of an open kiosk, construction of a temporary loose box at the stable, fabrication and installation of solar-powered street lights at the parade ground.
4.	The CMG remains steadfast in maintaining the status of Depot NA Osogbo as a befitting military institution through continuous maintenance and improvement of all infrastructure, thereby enhancing welfare of recruits and staff and overall training effectiveness.
departments.hod_bio:
Lt Col Ishaya Shehu Lucas was born on 2 Aug 83 to the family of Late
WOII Lucas Shehu (RTD) and Mrs Esi Hadiza Lucas. He hails from Karim
Lamido LGA of Taraba State. Ishaya started his primary education at Army
Children School Yola, in 1987 and gained admission into Vocational
Training Centre, Jalingo in 1994. In 1997, he proceeded to government
Science Secondary School, Jalingo where he obtained Senior School
Certificate in 2000. In 2001, he gained admission into Federal University of
Technology Yola, where he obtained Bachelor of Technology in
Architecture.
Lt Col Lucas was commissioned into the Nigerian Army on 6 Apr 11 as
a member of DSSC 18 which he was later converted to DRC 18 in 2013.
Courses he attended includes;
a.	Engineers Young Officers’ Course at NASME Makurdi in 2012.
b.	Bomb Disposal Officers’ Course at NASME Makurdi in 2015.
c.	FBI Post Blast Forensic Investigation Course at Police Bomb
School Ikeja in 2016.
d. 	International Improvised Explosive Device Disposal at DEMS
Regiment, Kineton, United Kingdom.
e.	Construction Management Officer’s Course at NASME Makurdi
in 2019.
f.	Senior Course 45 at AFCSC, Jaji from 2022 – 2023.
g.	Engineer Officers’ Squadron Commander Course at NASME
Makurdi in 2024.
Lt Col Lucas is happily married to Mrs Sandra Lami Ishaya and their
union is blessed with Three (3) children (2 Boys and a Girl). His hobbies
include playing basketball, golfing, traveling as well as watching
interesting TV shows.

departments.name: Drill Wing
departments.hod_bio:
1.	My name is Aondona Dominic Dowgo. I was born on 14 Apr 88 in Gboko LGA, I am Tiv by tribe from Vandeikya LGA of Benue State to the family of Chief BM Dowgo & Elizabeth Kwamande.
2.	During my Nur & Pri Edu, I attended Royal Kindergarten Nur & Pri Schl & LGEA Pri Sch in Makurdi Benue State. I further attended FGC Vandeikya in my secondary Edu. I did a direct entry program from the College of Advance & Professional Studies in Makurdi before proceeding to Ben Ste University 200 Level to Study B.Sc Hons Econs in 2008 for my Tertiary Edu.
3.	In 2009 I got admitted to the NDA as a member of 61 RC at the Academy. I studies B.Sc Hons Econs in The NDA & pass out in 2014. I was posted to the NAAC & served in the following units as a Commissioned officer :
a.  242 Recce Bn Badagry in 2015
b.  242 Recce Bn Mongunoi n 2016
c.  HQ NAAC Admin Bn Bauchi in 2020
d.  AHQ Gar Admin Bn Abj in 2023
e.   Depot NA Osogbo in 2025
4.	I attended my Corps career courses up to Armr Coy Comd Cse & other professional & career building Courses.
5.	During the course of my service as an Offr I got married on 14 Jul 18 while serving in 242 Bn Monguno & remain married with a Son.
6.	The above brief of My Life as a Civilian & Serving personnel in the NA is forwarded for your info & acquaintance Sir. Pse Ack///.
AD DOWGO
Osogbo								Maj
Jul 25									Ag SI Drill Wg

departments.name: Education Department
departments.hod_bio:
EARLY LIFE AND EDUCATION
1.	Maj Ibrahim Adamu Tahir was born in Kaduna on 8 February 1988 into the family of Alhaji Adamu Tahir. He hails from Gombe Local  Government Area of Gombe State. Ibrahim had his primary education at LGEA Sheikh Abubakar Mahmud Gumi Primary School and secondary education at Netherlands International Schools, both in Kaduna. He then proceeded to Kaduna Polytechnic Kaduna where he bagged a National Diploma in Mineral Resources Engineering in 2007. Thereafter, he gained admission into Usmanu Danfodiyo University Sokoto where he bagged a Bachelor’s of Science degree in Geography in 2011.
2.	Ibrahim also holds a Post Graduate Diploma in Education from National Teachers Institute Kaduna which He obtained in 2015 and a Masters of Science Degree in Geography from Kaduna State University which He obtained in 2025. He is also a member of Association of Nigerian Geographers, Teachers Registration Council of Nigeria and Nigerian Army Resource Centre.
MILITARY CAREER
3.	Ibrahim was commissioned to the rank of Lieutenant into the Nigerian Army Education Corps (NAEC) on 5 July 2014 as a member of Direct Short Service Commission Course 21. He served as Instructor at Nigerian Military School Zaria and Command Science Secondary School (Girls) Gusau from 2014 to 2017 and from 2017 to 2018 respectively. He also served as a Lecturer at the Nigerian Army College of Education Ilorin from 2020 to 2021.
4.	In July 2021, Ibrahim was posted to Depot Nigerian Army Zaria as Supervisory Education Officer, a post He held for eleven months before He was posted to 27 Taskforce Bde Buni Gari, Gujba LGA of Yobe State as a Watchkeeper in 2022. Thereafter, He was posted to Martin Luther Agwai International Leadership and Peacekeeping Centre Jaji as Instructor in January 2025. He currently serves at Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo as Supervisory Education Officer wef 6 April 2026.
5.	Ibrahim has attended several military courses commensurate with his rank. These include Young Officers course Education, Young Officers course Infantry, Basic Library and Advanced Library courses. Others are Basic Systems Approach to Training and Curriculum course, Basic NAEC Officers Development course, Junior Leadership and Staff Officers course and United Nations Military Observers Course among several others. He has participated in OPERATION HADIN KAI from July 2022 to January 2025
MARITAL STATUS AND HOBBIES
6.	Ibrahim is happily married to Bilkisu Yunusa Adam. The union is blessed with a b

departments.name: Finance Department
departments.hod_bio:
Major Musa Myek was born on 3rd April 1980 to the Family of Mr and Mrs Myek Magaji Dah in Jaba LGA of Kaduna State. He attended Mallam Maude Primary School, Kwoi where he obtained First School Leaving Certificate 1991. The Officer proceeded to Government Secondary School Fadan – Kaje in Kaduna State where he obtained O’Level Certificate in 1995. Thereafter, got admitted into Nuhu Bammali Polythetic Zaria were he obtained Higher National Diploma in Accounting (HND) in 2005.
Major Myek served as a corps member of the National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) at the Office of Accountant General of the Federation, Gombe State between the period of 2006 -2007. He further went to Nigerian College of Accountancy, Jos where he   obtained Certified National Account (CNA) in 2009.
He joined the Nigerian Army on 4th January 2014 as a member of Direct Short Service Commission DSSC 21 and was commission as Lieutenant on the 5th of July 2014 into the Nigerian Army Finance Corps.
Major Myek had served in various Units, such as the Nigerian Army School of Finance and Administration (NASFA) Lagos as an Instructor from 2014 – 2015, Nigerian Army Welfare Insurance Scheme (NAWIS) as Administrative Officer (AO) from 2015 – 2016, Nigerian Army College of Environmental Science and Technology (NACEST) as Staff Officer from 2016 – 2017, Nigerian Army Post Exchange Limited (NAPEX) as Finance Manager 2017 – 2019, 42 Engineers Brigade as Brigade Finance Officer (BFO) 2019 – 2021, HQ Car Lokoja as Acting Deputy Director Finance from Nov 21 – Feb 2022. Head Quarters 8 Division Finance as Acting Staff Officer 2 Payment (SO2 Pmt) from 2022 – 2023, Head Quarters Directorate of Legal Service Army (HQ DLS A) as Finance Officer (FO) 2024, after which he was posted to Depot NA Osogbo as pioneer Finance Officer (FO).
Major Musa also participated in Operation Hadin Kai (OPHK).
Myek has Attended several courses and Seminar, these include; Young Officer Course Finance (YOC) 2016, Computer Appreciation Course 2019, Staff Officer 2 Payment Course (SO2 Pmt) 2021, Junior Leadership and Staff Officer Course (JLSOC) 2024, 10th Nigerian Army Finance Corps Biennial Training Conference 2015, 14th Nigerian Army Finance Corps Biennial Training Conference 2024.  He is a Certified National Accountant (CNA) and Fellow Institute of Leadership and Human Resource (ILHR).
Musa is happily married to Mrs Mercy Myek Magaji. The Officer enjoys swimming, watching football and listening

departments.name: Headquarters Depot Nigerian Army
departments.brief: Headquarters Depot Nigerian Army, Osogbo is the command and administrative nerve centre of the Depot. It coordinates all operational, training, administrative and logistical activities of the formation. The headquarters is headed by the Commandant, a senior officer responsible for the overall management, discipline and welfare of all personnel and recruits under the Depot Nigerian Army.
departments.hod_bio:
Brigadier General Buhari Michael Madaki is a member of 47 Regular Course of the Nigerian Defence Academy and Commissioning Course 001 of the Royal Military Academy Sandhurst. He is of the Infantry Corps and has attended all military courses commensurate to his rank in the Infantry corps as well as several professional courses during his career. Some of these include the Higher Defence and Strategic Studies Course at the National Defence College Abuja (September 2022 to September 2023, Higher Military Strategy and Management Course at the Army War College Nigeria (April to December 2018 and Infantry Commanding Officer’s Course at the Nigerian Army School of Infantry Jaji (September to December 2016). He is a holder of master’s degrees in International Affairs and Strategic Studies from the Nigerian Defence Academy as well as International Defence and Security from Cranfield University.
Madaki has served in various capacities cutting across instruction, staff, command and diplomacy. Some of these include instructor at the Airborne Wing of the Nigerian Army School of Infantry (2005 to 2008) and Company Commander 4 Guards Battalion (2008 to 2010). He was Military Assistant to the Commandant of the Nigerian Defence Academy (2011 to 2012) and Acting Commanding Officer 72 Special Forces Battalion (2013 to 2014). He was privileged to serve as Directing Staff at the Military Training Assistance Programme Junior Command and Staff Course in Canada (2007), Armed Forces Command and Staff College Jaji (2016 to 2018) and National Defence College Abuja (2024 to 2025). He also served as Defence Attaché to Mali with concurrent accreditation to Senegal, The Gambia, Mauritania, Cape Verde and Western Sahara (2019 to 2022).
Madaki is the recipient of the Overseas Sword of Honour from the Royal Military Academy Sandhurst. His other awards and decorations include the United Nations Mission in Liberia medal, Nigeria Centenary and Golden Jubilee medals as well as General Operations and Foreign Training Assistance medals. Others are the Royal Military Academy Sandhurst and Command medals. Having attended the Advanced Airborne Course and qualified as a jump master, Buhari is passionate about the Airborne and strives to remain physically and mentally fit always. He is an alumnus of the Harvard Kennedy School and holder of an executive certificate in public leadership. He is a wildlife enthusiast and enjoys jogging and watching movies. His philosophy is hinged on the principle of good

departments.name: Islamic Affairs
departments.brief:
11.	MANDATES OF DEPORT NA ISLAMIC AFFAIRS OSOGBO
The mandates of Depot NA Islamic Affairs Osogbo is in line with the vision of the Directorate of Islamic Affairs Nigerian Army, which is to lead a crop of decent DOIA personnel who provide moral and spiritual guidance to Nigerian Army troops and their families for effective performance in line with COAS command philosophy. The mandates of Depot NA IA also include:
Giving spiritual advice to Depot Commandant on Islamic matters.
Leading officers, men, families and recruits Friday prayer service and the five daily prayers.
Spiritual guidance to officers, men, families and recruits.
Naming ceremony and prayers for new born babies.
Conducting wedding ceremony of troops.
Funeral prayers and burial of personnel, families and recruits.
Guidance and counseling o troops, families and recruits.
Spiritual healing if the need arises.
12.	CHALLENGES TO DEPOT NA ISLAMIC AFFAIRS OSOGBO
The challenges to Depot NA Islamic Affairs Osogbo includes the following:
Lack of frequent water supply in the Central Mosque.
No corpse washing facilities.
Praying carpets are not sufficient in the main Mosque hall and the female section.
There is no pulpit.
No Islamic books for research.
No teaching facilities for Depot Muslim women and their children.
The Central Mosque lacks a standard Imam Office and orderly room.
Short of manpower.
The central Mosque has no female soldiers that will be guiding and watching over the activities of the female recruits.
We need more tables and chairs.
The central Mosque needs more Air conditioners and a set of computer for the orderly room.
The central Mosque needs a set of plastic chairs and tables for Islamic events.
departments.hod_bio:
Capt AB Dikko N/16657, was born into the family of Barrister Dikko Isma’ila Dikko on the 17 Apr 85. I attended Tutuala Nursery and Primary School, Donga Local Government Area of Taraba State from 1992-1998 where he obtained his leaving School Certificate and Testimonial. Thereafter, diko gained admission into Federal Government College Wukari (FGCW) from 1998-2002, he was later transferred to Command Day Secondary School Kaduna from 2002-2006 where he obtained my Secondary School Certificate after successfully written my WAEC and NECO Exams.  After writing JAMB and gotten the required scores, he gained admission into the prestigious University of Maiduguri, Department of Arabic and Islamic Studies where he obtained BA (Hons) in Islamic Studies. After his mobilization by the University authority 2014, he was posted to Ebonyi State for the one year service to the nation through National Youth Service Corps NYSC.
3.	In 2016, Diko was among the successful citizens that were shortlisted for the Direct Short Service Commission Course 23 (DSSC/23).    After six months intensive military training at the Nigerian Army School of Infantry, Jaji Kaduna State. In June 2017,he was commissioned into the Nigerian Army as a Lieutenant and posted to the Directorate of Islamic Affairs as an Imam. He has attended Leadership and Regimentation in Serti Baruwa, 2017, Leadership Course at Nigerian Army Resource Centre Abuja, 2018, Imam YOC, 2019, Islamic Guidance and Counselling, 2021, Advance Islamic Guidance and Counselling, 2022, British Military Advisory Training (BMATT), 2024, Introduction to the Science of Hadith, 2025,	BMATT refresher course, 2025 and Junior Leadership and Staff Officers Course, 2025.
Diko has served in 301 AR(GA) Gombe State, as Regiment Imam in 2017-2018, 7 Guards Battalion, Abuja, 	Nigerian Army School of Islamic Affairs, as an Instructor and currently Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo, as Depot Imam as well as the pioneer Imam. He is married and the union is blessed with kids at his leisure, he enjoys Reading, Research,  Interacting with Scholars, Admonition and Travelling.

departments.name: Light Aid Detachment
departments.brief:
BRIEF ON THE ACTIVITIES OF THE DEPOT LIGHT AID DETACHMENT OSOGBO
INTRODUCTION
1.	The Depot Nigerian Army Light Aid Detachment (LAD) was established in Jun 25 as conveyed vide NAEME/G4/100/183 dated 17 Jun 25, with the primary role of providing equipment support to Depot NA Osogbo. To achieve this role the Detachment was promptly equipped to ensure timely and effective equipment maintenance in order to optimise equipment serviceability. The Detachment current manpower strength is 1 x 9.
ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES OF DEPOT NA OSOGBO LAD
2.	The statuary responsibilities of Depot LAD are to maintain Depot’s electrical, mechanical, optical and electro- medical equipment.  The LAD also provides technical advice to the Commandant on EME matters.
ACHVIMENTS
3.	Since its establishment the LAD has successfully executed several tasks including:
a.	Routine servicing of vehicles and power generating sets.
b.	Fabrication of   29 falling plates.
c.	Repair of 2 unserviceable Turismo busses.
d.	Refurbishment of the Review officer’s inspection Vehicle used for 98 RRI POP.
e.	Reinforcement and repair of Depot’s tentages.
f.	Recovery duties.
g.	Retrofitting of Toyota Hilux vehicle belonging to Osun state NYSC secretariat.
CO ODO
Maj
Jun 26								OC LAD
departments.hod_bio:
Major Odo Chinagorom Ogochukwu Williams was born on 18 Apr 1984 to the family of Mr and Mrs Williams Ugwuanyi of Amutenyi Obollo Eke in Udenu LGA of Enugu State. He attended Central School Obollo Afor were he obtained First School Leaving certificate in 1995. The Officer proceeded to Saint Teresa’s College Nsukka and obtained O’ Level certificate in 2021. Thereafter, got admitted into the prestigious University of Nigeria Nsukka were he obtained B. Engr Electronics   in 2008.  Maj Odo did compulsory National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) at Government Secondary School Serti Barwa in Gashaka LGA of Taraba State   from 2009 – 2010. He Joined the Nigerian Army on 15 Aug 2012 as a member of Short Service Combatant Course 41 and was commissioned as a 2Lt on 25 May 2013 into the Nigeria Army Electrical and Mechanical Engineers Corps.
Maj Odo has served in Command Workshop Mogadishu Cantonment Abuja – Pupilage from 2013 – 2015, 35 Arty Bde Workshop Alamala Abeokuta Ag Vehchle PL Com from 2016– 2018, 1041 Bde Workshop Ikeja Cantonment – Vehicle Pl Comd from Jan 2016 – Oct 2018, Nigerian Army Special Retrofitting and Repair Workshop (NASRRW) Ojo Cantonment Lagos – OC Repair Coy from Oct 2018 – Feb 2022, 25TF Bde Workshop Damboa Feb – Ag CO Bde Workshop        from 2022 – Nov 2022(Attached), 1033 Bde Workshop Gibson Jalo Cantonment Yola – Ag Production Officer from Nov 2022 – Jan 2024, 32 Arty Bde Workshop Owena Cantonment, Akure – Ag Production Officer Mar 2024 from where he reported to Depot NA Oshogbo as the Pioneer Officer Commanding Light Aid Detachment.
Maj CO Odo has attended several courses and seminars in the course of his career, these include; Basic Counter Terrorism cse Kontagora, EME YOC NASEME Auchi, Network Sy and Object Tracking DADP Lagos, Officers Armament and Instrument Course NASEME Auchi, Junior Staff Course AFCSC Jaji, Database Management System Course DADP Lagos, NAEME Median Seminar, Council for the Regulation of Engineering in Nigeria (COREN)   annual Seminars.  He is a registered member of COREN and Fellow Institute of Security Nigeria.
Maj Odo has participated in the following operations which include; Pl Comd COAS reserved Bn AHQ Gar for 2015 general elections, OC OP MESA covering Sagamu and Ogere under 35AB AOR and   sector 4 under 9Bde AOR, OP HARBIN KUNAMA II in Southern Kaduna, Op HADIN KAI as the ag CO Bde workshop 25TF Bde Damaboa,  Op Safe Conduct 2023 in

departments.name: Legal Department
departments.brief:
Legal Services
Depot NA Osogbo
Osogbo
Osun State
DEPOT NA/LS/G1/300/68					Email: dnao.ls@gmail.com
BRIEF ON THE ACTIVITIES OF THE LEGAL DEPARTMENT OF DEPOT NA OSOGBO
References:
A.	HQ DLS(A) G1 – 27 DTG 060950A FEB 26.
B.	MS/G1/300/207/2 dated 20 Feb 26.
INTRODUCTION
1.	I assumed duty as the Ag AD Legal of Depot NA Osogbo on 18 Feb 26 following my redeployment from HQ DLS (A) vide Reference A while Reference B subsequently formalized my posting.  Thus, on behalf of the Legal Department, I wish to appreciate the Comdt of Depot NA Osogbo for upholding the lofty objectives of the Training Institution. Additionally, gratitude is also expressed towards the commitment of the Comdt in catering for the welfare of officers and soldiers under command. The purpose of this brief therefore, is to highlight the activities of the Legal Department. The brief will cover provision of legal advice on agreements, legal reviews of reports of Boards of Inquiry (BOIs), Regimental Inquiries (RIs), Military Police Investigations (MPI) and delivery of lectures to troops and challenges of the unit (if any).
AIM
3.	The aim of this brief is to acquaint the Comdt of Depot NA Osogbo with the activities of the Legal Department.
PROVISION OF LEGAL ADVICE ON AGREEMENTS
5.	The Legal Department drafts and reviews contracts to protect the interest of Depot NA Osogbo. The Department identifies legal risks, ensure compliance with relevant laws and policies of regulatory agencies. It also clarifies obligations and provides strategic advice to help in safeguarding the objectives of the Institution.
LEGAL REVIEWS
6.	The Legal Department is responsible for the legal review of Military Police Investigation Reports, Board of Inquiries and Regimental Inquiries. From the period covering the first and second quarters of the year 2026, the unit has made several legal reviews which have attracted the approval of ASA.
DELIVERY OF LECTURES ON BASIC MILITARY LAW AND THE LAW OF ARMED CONFLICT
7.	The Legal Department in collaboration with the Depot NA Provost Coy from time to time deliver lectures on basic military law and the law of armed conflict with more emphasis in areas such as:
Basic Military Law.
Rules of Engagements.
The Status of Prisoners of War.
Difference between Military Law and Martial Law.
Peace Supports Operations amongst others law related subjects.
CHALLENGES
8.	The Legal Department does not have any serious challenges or issues confronting the Department at the moment. However, the Departm
departments.hod_bio:
1.	Capt EF Collins is an indigene of Sabatoru Town in Nembe Local Government Area of Bayelsa State. He was born to the family of Chief Collins Ekine Eyeke and Mrs Stella Ekine Collins. He is the first child of 3 children. He started his primary education in 1990 at Township Primary School, Moscow Road, Port Harcourt. On completion of his primary education, He was admitted into Baptist High School Borokiri Port Harcourt for his secondary education. He obtained his Senior Secondary School Certificate of Education in 2002. Thereafter, he gained admission into Niger Delta University, Bayelsa State where he obtained a Bachelor of Laws in 2010 and proceeded to the Nigerian Law School, Lagos Campus. He was called to the bar as a Barrister and Solicitor of the Supreme Court of Nigeria on November 25 2014.
2.	Capt Collins was commissioned Lieutenant having successfully completed his cadet training on 3 June 2017 as a member of the Direct Short Service Course 23. He was subsequently posted to 82 Division Legal Services as his mother unit. In August 2020, he was converted from Direct Short Service Commission to Direct Regular Commission. In the officer’s career that is approaching a decade of meritorious service, he has participated in several operations, served in different units and attended military courses commensurate to his rank. These are highlighted in the succeeding paragraphs.
OPERATIONAL EXPERIENCE
4.	Capt Collins has participated in several miliary operations including Operation Lafiya Dole which was later changed to Operation Hadin Kai in North East, Nigeria. Others are Operations Dominion Storm, Whirl Punch and Mesa all in North Central, Nigeria.
POSTING HISTORY
5.	Capt Collins after his commission, has served in several units and formations including 82 Division Legal Services Enugu, HQ Sector 3 Op Hadin Kai Monguno. Others are 31 Artillery Brigade Minna and HQ DLS (A) Abuja. While serving in these units, he was sometimes redeployed to other formations for court martial duties. This led to his appointment as a prosecutor at HQ 7 Division General Court Martial and also as Judge Advocate 1 Division General Court Marial. He currently the Acting Assistant Director, Legal of Depot Nigerian Army, Osogbo.
COURSES ATTENDED
6.	On commission as a Lieutenant, the officer attended Leadership and Regimentation Course from July to September 2017 at 20 Model Bn, Serti Baruwa, Taraba State. On completion of the course at 20 Mod

departments.name: Medical Centre
departments.brief: The Medical Centre, Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo provides primary and secondary healthcare services to all military personnel and their dependants within the Depot. The centre handles routine medical examinations for recruits on admission and throughout training, manages sick parades, emergency care, and coordinates medical evacuations. It is staffed by trained military medical officers and support personnel committed to maintaining the health and fitness of the Depot community.
departments.hod_bio:
MAJOR (DR) JONATHAN ANIETIE EDIMEK PJLSC, MBBS, MBA, MPH Bsc MCB ,Ag CO, Depot Nigerian Army Medical Centre, Osogbo
1.	Maj (Dr) Jonathan Anietie Edimek is military medical officer, healthcare administrator, public health researcher with over 26 years of meritorious service in the NA and more than 14 years of clinical medical practice, he has consistently demonstrated exceptional commitment to health care. Maj Edimek commenced his military career in October 1999 after passing out of the prestigious NMS zaria, and subsequently earned his Bachelor of science in Microbiology and then a Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) degree from the University of Uyo and University of Calabar in 2005 and 2011 respectively. He completed his Housemanship at the University of Uyo Teaching Hospital in 2012 before joining the Nigerian Army Medical Corps as a member of DSSC 21 in 2014. He further enhanced his professional competence by obtaining an MBA from Ahmadu Bello University, Zaria, in 2023 and a Masters in Public Health (MPH) at the same institution.
2.	Throughout his military career, Maj Edimek has served in numerous operational, regimental, national, and international appointments. He served with the ECOWAS Mission in The Gambia (ECOMIG), the United Nations-African Union Mission in Darfur (UNAMID). He served as Team Lead for the Defence Headquarters COVID-19 North-West Response. Major Edimek has also held appointments at the Military Hospital Lagos (MHL), 242 Bn Konduga, 5 bde Konduga, Military Hospital Sokoto, 212Bn Konduga during Operations DEEP PUNCH II and RESCUE FINALE, 243 Bn Badagry, 15 Field Engineer Regiment badagry, 12 FER Ijebuode,1 Bn Birnin Kebbi, Defence Space Administration Clinic Abuja, and currently serves as the Ag CO, Depot Nigerian Army Medical Centre, Osogbo.
3.	As the pioneer CO of the newly established Depot NA Med Cen, Osogbo, Major Edimek has overseen a remarkable transformation of the facility from its formative stage into a functional and expanding healthcare institution supporting recruit training and military healthcare delivery . Beyond clinical practice and military leadership, Major Edimek is an accomplished researcher whose work focuses on public health, disease prevention, healthcare access, and health systems strengthening. His notable research includes studies on malaria prevention practices in urban and rural communities of Abuja, the impact of cashless policy implementation in Nigeria, and collaborative research in o

departments.name: Military Intelligence Regiment
departments.brief: The Military Intelligence Regiment (MIR) at Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo is responsible for gathering, analysing and disseminating intelligence relevant to the security and operations of the Depot. The MIR supports the Commandant with threat assessments, counter-intelligence activities and coordination with higher intelligence formations of the Nigerian Army.
departments.hod_bio:
1.	Captain Abdulkadir Abubakar Bindawa was born on 20 April 1991 at Bindawa town into the family of Mr and Mrs Alh Kado Bature Bindawa. He hails from Bindawa Local Government Area of Katsina State. He had his primary education at Bindawa Pilot Primary School and proceeded to Government Science Secondary School Bindawa for his secondary education. He gained admission to study Computer Science at Alqalam University, Katsina and graduated with a Bachelor of Science in Computer Science.
2.	 He was selected for officer cadet training at the Nigerian Defence Academy as a member of Short Service Combatant Course 44. He was commissioned Second Lieutenant on 16 September 2017 and posted to Nigerian Army Intelligence Corp. As an officer, he has served as an instructor at the Nigerian Army Intelligence School Apapa, Lagos and Officer in Charge of Special Intelligence Cell at Special Intelligence Cell Damaturu, Yobe State.  He equally served as Acting Commanding Officer 27 Task Force Brigade Buni Gari, Yobe State and he is currently serving as Acting Commanding Officer Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo Military Intelligence Regiment.

departments.name: Motor Transport Yard
departments.brief: The Motor Transport Yard (MT Yard) manages the fleet of vehicles belonging to the Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo. Its responsibilities include the maintenance, servicing and deployment of military vehicles in support of training activities, administrative movements and operational tasks. The MT Yard ensures all vehicles are roadworthy and available for use at all times.
departments.hod_bio:
1.	Major Habib Habib Bello was born on 22 Feb 1985 to the family of Alhaji Bello Habib. He hails from Gwale Local Govt of Kano State and was commissioned into the Nigerian Army as a Second Lieutenant on 8 September 2012 as a member of Short Service Course 40.
2.	He began his primary school education at St Louis Nursery and Primary School Kano from 1993 – 1997 and thereafter proceeded to ST Thomas Secondary School where he obtained West African Senior Secondary School Leaving Certificate in the year 2003. He then proceeded to Bayaro University Kano where he obtained a B.Sc. in Political Science in the year 2010.
3.	Major Bello has attended the following courses; Young Officer Course Supply and Transport. Mechanical Transport Officers Course, Unarmed Combat Course and Junior Staff Course at Armed Forces Command and Staff College.
4.	Major Bello has participated in the following operations; Operation Burst, Operation Zama Lafiya, Operation Lafiya Dole, Operation Delta Safe and Operation Safe Heaven. Major Bello is currently the Ag CO Transport Battalion at Depot NA Osogbo. Major Bello is widower blessed with three children.

departments.name: Provost Company
departments.hod_bio:
PROFILE OF CAPTAIN OLUBODUN MICHAEL ODENIRAN
1.	Captain Olubodun Michael Odeniran was born on 31 December 1986 at Ibadan into the family of Mr and Mrs Olu Odeniran. He hails from Ife -East Local Government Area of Osun State. He had his primary education at Local Education Authority Primary School Kigo Road, Kaduna and proceeded to Nigerian Military School Zaria for his secondary education. He gained admission to study Civil Law at Nasarawa State University Keffi and graduated with a Bachelor of Laws. Thereafter, he proceeded to Nigerian Law School Yola Campus and was called to the Nigerian Bar as a Barrister and Solicitor of the Supreme Court of Nigeria on 28 November 2013.
2.	Upon conclusion of his training as a boy soldier, he joined the Nigerian Army on 4 
August 2001 and was posted to Headquarters Guards Brigade at Aguyi Ironsi Cantonment Asokoro, Abuja. Subsequently, he was deployed to 177 Guards Battalion Shittu Alao Barracks Keffi, Nasarawa State where he served from 2001 – 2007. He also served at 2 Division Garrison and 2 Division Legal Services at Adekunle Fajuyi Cantonment Ibadan Oyo State 
from 2007 - 2014. Being a legal practitioner, he served as a prosecutor at 1 Division General Court Martial under supervision of 1 Division Legal Services Dalet Barracks Kawo, Kaduna.
3.	While serving at the court martial, he was selected for officer cadet training at the Nigerian Defence Academy as a member of Short Service Combatant Course 43. He was commissioned Second Lieutenant on 15 September 2015 and posted to Nigerian Army Corps of Military Police. As an officer, he has served as an instructor at the Nigerian Army School of Military Police Basawa Zaria and Platoon Commander at 6 Division Provost Group Port Harcourt.  He equally served as Acting Staff Officer Grade 2 Legal and Vetting at Headquarters, Nigerian Army Corps of Military Police and he is currently serving as the Pioneer Acting Officer Commanding Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo Provost Company.
4.	In his quest for more knowledge, he obtained a Master of Laws from Ahmadu Bello University Zaria. He also engaged in professional security training and qualified as a Certified Security Supervisor and Certified Chief Security Officer of the International Security Organisation Switzerland. He is happily married to Mrs Temitope Elizabeth Odeniran and he 
has 2 kids namely; Mofiyinfoluwa and Oluwanifemi.

departments.name: Physical Training Wing
departments.brief:
BRIEF ON THE RESPONSIBILITIES OF DEPOT NIGERIAN ARMY OSOGBO PHYSICAL TRAINING WING
INTRODUCTION
1.  	The Physical Training (PT) Wing of Depot Nigerian Army (NA), Osogbo, is a critical component of the institution responsible for the planning, coordination, supervision, and execution of all physical training activities for personnel and recruits. As physical fitness remains an essential requirement for military effectiveness, the wing is tasked with developing and sustaining the physical, mental, and psychological readiness of soldiers through structured training programmes. These programmes are designed to enhance strength, endurance, agility, stamina, discipline, resilience, and combat efficiency, thereby enabling personnel and recruits to effectively meet the physical demands of military service. The wing also provides professional guidance on fitness standards and ensures that all physical training activities are conducted in accordance with established Nigerian Army policies and training directives.
2.	In addition to routine physical training, the PT Wing organizes and supervises a wide range of fitness assessments, sporting activities, and competitive events aimed at promoting teamwork, leadership, esprit de corps, and healthy competition among personnel and recruits. These activities include the conduct of morning physical training, evening games, Bi-Annual Physical Efficiency Tests (BPET), endurance race training and competitions, obstacle training and competitions, novices boxing bouts, circuit training, sports week competitions, unarmed combat and self-defence training, as well as the EX ETI PARI Dash Back exercise. Through these activities, the wing contributes significantly to the overall training objectives of Depot Nigerian Army, Osogbo, by producing physically fit, mentally alert, and combat-ready soldiers capable of undertaking the diverse operational responsibilities of the Nigerian Army. These activities shall be discussed below with its challenges and proffer solutions.
AIM
3.	The aim of this brief is to acquaint the Commandant Depot NA Osogbo on the Responsibilities of Depot NA Osogbo Physical Training Wing.
POSTED STRENGTH
4.	The Physical Training Wing of Depot NA, Osogbo, has a posted strength of 2 x 43 Soldiers.
CONDUCT OF MORNING PT AND EVENING GAMES.
5.	Depot Nigerian Army, Osogbo, conducts morning PT every Monday and Wednesday also evening games every Tuesday and Thursday to maintain the overall physical fitness of its personnel. T
departments.hod_bio:

The senior officer attended the	Basic Physical Training Course, Advance Physical Training Course, Probation Physical Training Course, Young Officers Physical Training Course (YOC PT), Officers Sports Management Course, Basic Computer Course, the Conduct of Humanitarian Relief Operation, Principle of Intervention and Management Course and the introduction to United Nation Mission, Field Operators Course.

The senior officer participated in African Mission in Sudan (AMIS), United Nation High Breed Forces in Dafur (UNHBD), United Nation Mission in Sierra-leon (UNAMSIL), Operation Mesa, Operation Crocodile Smile, 	Joint Task Force North West Operation Fansan Yamma, Operation Thunder Storm and Operation Polo Shield.
6.	The senior officer was on time the Sports Officer Physical Training (SOPT) in Depot NA Zaria, Sports Officer Physical Training (SOPT) in 4 Brigade NA Benin Edo, Sector Commander Operation Thunder Storm, Sports Officer Physical Training (SOPT) in Defence Intelligent Agency 	Abuja, Sports Officer Physical Training (SOPT) in HQ Nigerian Army Corp of 	Artillery Kontagora,

departments.name: Quartermaster Department
departments.brief:
BRIEF ON THE QUARTER MASTER DEPARMENT DEPOT NIGERIAN ARMY OSOGBO
1.	The Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo Quartermaster (QM) Department has been one of the backbones in the training of Recruits in Depot NA OS. It is also the logistics Dept in charge of issuance of stores, accommodation, arms and ammunition, other tasks are receipt, account, and maintenance of stores, arms and ammunition in storage.  The Dept issues stores, arms and ammo for the training and kitting of recruit as it strives to achieve the Comdt’s vision which is to provide functional military training and orientation to Recruit, transforming them into competent, disciplined and loyal soldiers for the Nigerian Army.
2.	The QM Dept Depot NA OS was formed following the establishment of Depot NA OS on 7 Jun 25. The dept composed of one officer from inception. However, with subsequent postings and internal redeployments, the dept’s manpower holding has raised to 2 x 16 soldiers at the moment. The QM Dept as one of the key branches in Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo is responsible for providing logistics and administrative support required for seamless recruit training and daily activities. The department is listed as one of the major components of Depot NA's organizational structure.
3. 	Since the establishment of Depot NA Osogbo, the dept has carried out its primary responsibilities which includes, receipt, Supply and Distribution, Procurement, maintenance, issuance of uniforms, boots, beddings, training equipment, stationeries and other military equipment and stores. Other roles includes, setting of camps for FTX, allocation of accommodation for both recruits and personnel’s and keeping of inventories.
4.	The dept remains steadfast in maintaining the status of Depot NA OS as a befitting military institution through continuous discharge of its duties by enhancing welfare of recruits and staffs overall training effectiveness.
departments.hod_bio:

departments.name: Records Office
departments.brief: The Records Office, Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo maintains comprehensive records of all military personnel who have served in or passed through the Depot. It handles documentation related to enlistment, posting, promotions, discharges and service history. The office plays a vital role in ensuring the accuracy and integrity of all personnel data within the Depot formation.
departments.hod_bio:
Lt Col Idowu Kayode Oladipo was born on 1 Apr 85 to the family of late Mr and late Mrs BO Oladipo. I hail from Okemesi in Ekiti-West LGA of Ekiti State. He attended Command Childrens’ School and Command Day Secondary School Ojo Mil Cant, Lagos state. He later proceeded to NDA Kaduna, as a member of 57 RC and posted to NACA upon commissioning. He hold a BSc in Geography from NDA and a MA in Leadership and Security Studies (in view). Notable amongst courses attended are YOC Arty, Troop Comd Course, Regimental Officers’ Gunnery Course, Battery Comd Course, Junior Comd and Staff Course in AFCSC and Senior Comd and Staff Course in AFCSC.
Idowu has served in Bty Comd/2iC 375 Arty Regt Baga and Mallam Fatori, Borno State, 	SRO Bauchi State, OC FOBs Zurmi, Tsafe, Talata Mafara and Gudu NW Theatre, erstwhile OPHD, and SO1 G7 HQ NACA. He has participated in UNAMID Ops Sudan, OP Restore Order 1&2, OP BOYONA, OP Zaman Lafiya, OP Lafiya Dole in NE, Borno state and OPHD in NW, Sokoto and Zamfara States.
He is married and my hubbies include; reading, listening to music and playing table tennis

departments.name: Recruits Centre
departments.brief: The Recruits Centre, Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo is responsible for receiving, processing and administering new recruits from the point of enlistment through their entire basic military training. The centre ensures that all recruits meet the physical, medical and administrative requirements for service in the Nigerian Army, and manages their welfare, discipline and documentation throughout the training period.
departments.hod_bio:
ACTING COMMANDER RECRUITS CENTRE – LIEUTENANT COLONEL EMMANUEL MANASSEH UKANDE
Lieutenant Colonel Emmanuel Manasseh Ukande is a member of Short Service Course 33 of the Nigerian Defence Academy and commissioned in 2003. The senior officer is of the Infantry Corps and has attended military courses commensurate to his rank in the Nigerian Army as well as other specialized warfare courses at Nigerian Army School of Infantry and abroad. Some of these include the Junior and Senior Courses at AFCSC Jaji (January -June 2015 and January 2019 – August 2020 respectively), Commanding Officers Course (January – April 2026), Company Commanders Course (September – December 2015) and Young Officers Course (February – May 2006). Others include Special Operations Command Course in People’s Liberation Army China at the Quanzhou Military Academy (August 2016 – July 2017), Basic and Advanced Airborne Courses at Airborne Wing Jaji (February – March 2005 and April – June 2026), Protection of Civilians Course at Martin Luther Agwai Leadership and Peace Keeping Centre Jaji (July – August 2016). The senior officer also attended United Nations Battalion Course in South Korea (November 2022).
Ukande has served and held several appointments cutting across staff, command and instruction in units and various operations. Some of these include Adjutant and Operations Officer at 192 Battalion (2003 – 2005), Platoon Commander in UNMIL (2006) and UNAMID (2007) respectively. Emmanuel was also an instructor and Administrative Officer at the Tactics Wing of the Nigerian Army School of Infantry (May – August 2016). Other appointments held by the officer include acting Sector Commander in Operations RESTORE ORDER III, BOYONA and ZAMAN LAFIYA (July 2011 – May 2016). He was the Training Officer at the Nigerian Army Special Forces School Buni Yadi (September 2020 – May 2021). He was privileged to command the 2 Special Women Operations Battalion Abakaliki (May 2021 – July 2023) and 196 Amphibious Battalion Baga in Operation HADIN KAI NORTH EAST/MULTINATIONAL JOINT TASK FORCE (July 2023 – May 2025
Ukande is the recipient of awards and decorations which include the Meritorious Service Star, Force Service Star, Passed Staff Course, Nigeria Centenary and Golden Jubilee medals as well as General Operations and Command medals. The officer enjoys working out and observing people. He also delights in mentoring subordinates and impacting knowledge and values. The senior officer is married with children. Lieu

departments.name: Signals Squadron
departments.brief:
BRIEF ON DEPOT NIGERIAN ARMY OSOGBO SIGNALS WING
INTRODUCTION
1.	Depot NA Osogbo Signals Wing was established following the Commencement of recruits training at Depot NA Osogbo on 6 Jun 25. The mandate of the Signals Wing is to provide effective and efficient communications support to Depot NA Osogbo and also instruct the recruits in Signals Communication as well as Information and Communication Technology. The Wing has been living up to its expectations since inception.
2.	The purpose of this brief is to highlight the activities of Depot NA Osogbo Signals Wing. The brief will cover administration, training, state of communications, challenges and achievements.
AIM
3.	The aim of this brief is to acquaint you with the activities 
of Depot NA Osogbo Signals Wing.
ADMINISTRATION
4.	The general administration in the Unit has been commendable since my assumption of duty as the Ag SI. The brief on administration will cover organization, state of manpower holding, discipline and welfare.
a.	Organization.	According to the NAS ORBAT 2023, a typical Signals Wing is organized into Signals Wing HQ, Communication Training Department and the Communication Support Department. Presently, the Wing, integrates these departments to function as single entity due to dearth of personnel. It is not yet set up in accordance with the ORBAT. notwithstanding, the wing has been able to efficiently function to carryout its assigned roles and responsibilities.
b.	State Of Manpower Holding.	The Strength of a Signals Wing according to the NAS ORBAT 2023 is 7 x 32 officers and soldiers. However, the Unit’s current strength is 1 x 9 officer and soldiers. This has sometimes posed challenges to effective instructional periods considering the large number of recruits and the instructors mostly involved in barrack duties and other routine engagements.
c.	Discipline.	The discipline of own personnel is of high standards, though minor cases reported were treated and disposed of appropriately. Instructors are regularly monitored to act professionally while discharging their responsibilities and assigned tasks. There is continuous monitoring of personnel’s conduct to ensure discipline and adherence to standards.
d.	Welfare.	The Signals Wing places a high priority on the welfare of its personnel, while recognizing the direct impact on morale. To this end, passes are issued promptly and soldiers are highly encouraged to attend career-advancing courses at the opportune times, with a view to fostering
departments.hod_bio:
1.	Capt Aliyu Muhammed Bello was born on 31 Dec 93 into the family of Mr and Mrs Bello Mohammed and hails from Birnin Kebbi Local Government Area of Kebbi State. He is the second of 4 children. Aliyu began his primary school Education 
in 1996 at Turaki International School Kaduna where he obtained First School Leaving Certificate in the year 2004. He further proceeded to Essence International School Kaduna where he obtained the West African Senior School Certificate in the year 2010. Capt Bello was subsequently admitted into the prestigious Nigerian Defence Academy Kaduna as a member of 64 Regular Course 
on 22 Sep 12. He later graduated with a Bachelor of Engineering in Electrical and Electronics Engineering in 2017.
2.	Capt AM Bello was commissioned into the NA as a Second Lieutenant on 16 
Sep 17 and posted to the Nigerian Army Signals Corps. He was subsequently posted to 535 Signals Regiment Jos where he participated in various competitions for the Regiment and won laurels. Bello was later posted to Headquarters Nigerian Army Signals Regiment Lagos where he served as the unit Adjutant for about 2 years. In Dec 22, he was posted to Headquarters Joint Task Force (North East) Operation Hadin Kai where he served successfully until Mar 25 when he proceeded to 562 Brigade Signals Yenagoa. Having served in Yenagoa for about 3 months, Bello was subsequently posted to Depot NA Osogbo as a member of the pioneer team mandated to train competent soldiers for the NA.
3.	During the course of his career, Aliyu attended various military courses commensurate to his rank. They include Young Officers Course Signals, Young Officers Course Infantry, Reverse Engineering Malware Analysis and Open-Source Intelligence Courses amongst others. Similarly, Aliyu attended Obafemi Awolowo University Ile-Ife where he obtained a Master of Technology degree in Telecommunication and Electronics Engineering. Capt Bello has also attended Junior Course at the Armed Forces Command and Staff College Jaji.
4.	Capt AM Bello is happily married. During his leisure, he enjoys reading history,
current affairs, playing video games, squash and watching football.
AM BELLO
Osogbo								Capt
Jun 26								Ag SI Sigs Wg

departments.name: Tactics Wing
departments.hod_bio:
BIOGRAPHY MAJ AD MOHAMMED
Maj AD Mohammed (N/13981) was born on 9 June 1985 to the family of Late Alh and Haj Hauwau Mohammed Bida in Kaduna State. He hails from Bida LGA of Niger State. He obtained his first school living certificate in LEA Katsina road primary school Kaduna between 1990 and 1996. He attended Anny secondary school Kaduna between 1997 and 2002.
He proceeded to Kaduna polytechnic where he obtained a National and Higher National Diploma in Mass communication. He was enlisted into the Nigerian Army on the 10 August 2010 and completed his cadet training on 6 April 2011 as a member of Short Service Course 39.

At the end of his cadet training, Maj AD Mohammed was posted to ICC and subsequently to 103 Bn as a platoon commd. Other units he has served includes, AHQ Special Ops Bn, 133 SF Bn, 65 Bn, 81 Div TF Bn, 5 Bn and now Depot as the Ag SI Tactics. Some of the Ops he participated include, Op Zaman Lafiya, Ops Lafiya Dole, Op Tura Takai Bango, Op Rescue Finale, Op Deep Punch II, Op Harbin Kunama, Op MESA, Op Hadin Kai and Op Delta safe amongst others.
Maj AD Mohammed has attended the following couses. Special Warfare course (Tracker), Young officers course infantry, Anti-Tank platoon comd course, Amoury management course, Junior Comd and Staff Course and Company Comd Course. Maj Mohammed has the following medals and awards. OPLD, OPHK, GOM, FSS and PJSC. His hobbies include playing basketball, Table tennis, and badminton. Maj AD Mohammed is married and the union is blessed with 3 kids.

departments.name: Training Branch
departments.brief: The Training Branch, Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo is the central coordinating authority for all recruit training programmes within the Depot. It designs, supervises and evaluates the military training curriculum delivered to recruits, ensuring compliance with Nigerian Army training standards and directives. The branch coordinates with all training wings — Drill, Weapons, Tactics, Physical Training and Signals — to produce well-trained soldiers fit for operational duties.

departments.name: Weapons Wing
departments.hod_bio:
MAJ ATTAH VALENTINE CHIJIOKE WAS BORN ON 16 JULY 1975 IN OHAMAKA, AGBAMERE, EHA-ALUMONAH IN NSUKKA LGA OF ENUGU STATE TO THE FAMILY OF MR AND MRS CHARLES  ATTAH. HE ATTENDED STATE PRIMARY SCHOOL AGBAMERE COMMUNITY HIGH SCHOOL UMABOR EHA-ALUMONA AND NUHU BAMALLI POLYTECHNIC ZARIA.
MAJ VALENTINE JOINED THE NIGERIAN ARMY IN THE YEAR 1996 AS  A MEMBER OF 43 RRI AND WAS COMMISSIONED IN 2013 AS A MEMBER OF EXECUTIVE  COMMISSION 1,13. HE ATTENDED YOC INF - JAJI, APC PL COMD - JAJI, BASIC RGE MGT - JAJI AND BASIC OPERATING CSE -  MAKURDI.
VALENTINE HAS PARTICIPATED IN ECOMOG PEACE KEEPING IN LIB AND SIERRA LEONE,
OP HARMONY BAKASSI PENINSULA, AMIS PROTECTION FORCE DAFUR REGION IN SUDAN, ZAMAN LAFIYA NE, LAFIYA DOLE NE, HARDIN KAI NE, FOREST SANITY 111 NW
FASAN YAMAN NW.
H.E HAS SERVED IN DEPOT NA ZARIA, 174 BN, AHQ GAR, 6BN, 101 SF BN, 5BN, 45BN AND DEPOT NA OSOGBO. HE IS MARRIED AND BLESSED WITH CHILDREN.
